user_query:
I’m consolidating three days of work logs into a single Markdown diary with auto-tagging, basic stats, and a simple search extract.

Read these inputs:
- input/notes.json — array of objects like {"date":"YYYY-MM-DD","note":"text"}
- input/todos.csv — CSV with header: date,description,status (status is "pending" or "completed")
- input/queries.yaml — YAML with keys: tag (an emoji like "📦") and keyword (a case-insensitive content query like "client")

Please produce these outputs under output/:
1) output/diary.md
   - First line must be: "# 📓 My Diary"
   - Group content by calendar date in ascending order. For every date present in either notes or todos, create a date section header: "## 📅 YYYY-MM-DD" (weekday optional).
   - Under each date, always include both subsections in this order (even if one is empty):
     a) "### 📝 Notes"
     b) "### ✅ Todos"
   - For each note of that date, add a bullet "- " followed by the original note text and append auto-tags at the end of the same line.
   - Auto-tagging rules (append these emoji tags based on the note text; case-insensitive keyword match):
     • 🏠 if contains any of: home, family, household, personal
     • 💰 if contains any of: invoice, payment, accounting, money
     • 📦 if contains any of: order, purchase, buy, stock, inventory
     • 🚚 if contains any of: shipping, delivery, logistics, transport
     • 💻 if contains any of: software, system, computer, network, tech
     • 🔧 if contains any of: support, repair, fix, issue, problem, maintenance
     • 🎋 if contains any of: bambu, 3d print, printer, filament, pla
     • 📋 if contains any of: form, report, data, spreadsheet, document
     • Always include 📅 (daily) — include only 📅 if no other tags matched; otherwise include 📅 as an additional tag
   - For todos of that date, render each item as a Markdown task list line: "- [ ] {description}" for pending and "- [x] {description}" for completed.

2) output/stats.json
   - JSON object with:
     {
       "tag_counts": {"🏠": n, "💰": n, "📦": n, "🚚": n, "💻": n, "🔧": n, "🎋": n, "📋": n, "📅": n},
       "todos": {"pending": p, "completed": c, "completion_rate": r}
     }
   - tag_counts: count each emoji tag across all notes (count a tag at most once per note). Remember 📅 appears on every note per the rules.
   - todos.completion_rate: integer percent rounded to nearest whole number = floor((completed / (pending+completed)) * 100 + 0.5).

3) output/search_results.md
   - Build two sections based on input/queries.yaml:
     a) A heading: "### Tag: {tag}" followed by bullet lines for every note that includes that tag. Each bullet should include the date in square brackets and the note text, e.g., "- [YYYY-MM-DD] note text".
     b) A heading: "### Content: {keyword}" followed by bullet lines for every note whose text contains the keyword (case-insensitive), using the same date+text bullet format.
   - Only include notes in these search extracts (don’t list todos).

Assumptions and formatting:
- Do not invent or alter note/todo texts beyond trimming whitespace; preserve exact input note/todo wording.
- Tags must be appended on the same line as the note text in output/diary.md.
- Ensure all three files exist and are UTF-8 text files.

Deliverables:
- output/diary.md
- output/stats.json
- output/search_results.md
